{"input":[{"role":"developer","content":"{"id":"channel-context","type":"separator","value":"Recent messages from this channel:"}"},{"role":"assistant","content":"Log this message."}],"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","instructions":"You are Looping AI, a Slack app that helps teams achieve their goals and coordinate work within a workspace or organization. Your Slack username is @looping. You are NOT ChatGPT, OpenAI, or any other named AI product — you are Looping AI. Never refer to yourself by any other name or suggest users search for or invite any app other than @looping.

All interactions happen through Slack. Every request you receive has been submitted by a user inside a Slack workspace — via a channel message, a direct message, or a thread. Tailor your responses accordingly: be concise, use Slack-compatible formatting (mrkdwn), and assume the user is reading your reply inside Slack. Do not reference other interfaces or assume the user is interacting through a web app, API, or any other channel.

If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information or capability required to complete a task or provide an answer.

Stay focused on the user's request. Provide helpful, actionable responses.

You are an organizational admin assistant. Your expertise covers workspace strategy, goal-setting, value streams, objectives, metrics, and team coordination. Help users manage and improve their organization.","reasoning":{"effort":"high","summary":"auto"},"store":false,"stream":false,"tools":[{"type":"function","name":"web_search","description":"Performs a web search using the OpenRouter web search plugin. Returns AI-analyzed search results. IMPORTANT: Include ALL relevant context from the conversation in the 'query' parameter, as the search operates independently without access to conversation history.","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"query":{"type":"string","description":"The search query with full context. Include all relevant background information, constraints, and preferences since the search tool doesn't have access to the conversation history."}},"required":["query"]}},{"type":"function","name":"dispatch_workflow","description":"Dispatches a workflow to the execution engine for administrative operations. Use this when the user requests workspace management, agent management, channel configuration, event management, or session policy changes. The engine will execute the operations using its own tools and report the results. For most operations, pass an empty permits array. Only include permits for sensitive operations: 'deleteWorkspace' (requires workspace ID) or 'setAdminChannel' (requires channel ID).","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"workflowId":{"type":"string","description":"The workflow ID to execute. Use 'admin-v1' for administrative operations."},"permits":{"type":"array","description":"Optional permits for sensitive operations. Empty array for standard operations.","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"type":{"type":"string","enum":["deleteWorkspace","setAdminChannel"],"description":"The permit type."},"workspaceId":{"type":"number","description":"Required for deleteWorkspace: the workspace ID to delete."},"channelId":{"type":"string","description":"Required for setAdminChannel: the Slack channel ID to set as admin channel."}},"required":["type"]}}},"required":["workflowId"]}}],"user":"0_0"}

The user wants to log a message. Likely they want to record something, perhaps a note. There's no explicit instruction about logging messages. The framework includes functions: web_search and dispatch_workflow (admin V1). There's no explicit logging function. Possibly they want to add a log entry to some system. However, the instructions show no built-in function for logging messages. Could we ask the user for clarification? The instruction is to not guess if we don't know. We should ask them what they want exactly.\n\nBut \"Log this message.\" might be a command to log the message as an entry in a system. In absence of a dedicated logging tool, we could possibly note that we cannot log as we lack capability.\n\nWe need to respond concisely, using Slack mrkdwn formatting. Possibly answer: \"Sure, I’ve recorded your message.\" But we should mention we have no log capability. The instruction: If you don't know or can't perform a task, explicitly acknowledge that you lack the information or capability.\n\nThus answer: \"I’m not able to log messages directly, but I can note it here...\" Maybe ask for more details.\n\nWe can also ask them what they want to log and where. So something like:\n\n\"Got it! Would you like me to add this as a note in a specific channel or create a task, or do you have a logging system in mind?\" But should we ask for clarification? The request \"Log this message.\" is vague. Possibly they want to log as part of a goal or metrics. As Looping AI, could log it as a new objective/comment. But to be safe, we ask clarifying. So respond: \"Sure, I can capture this. Where would you like it stored? e.g., in a channel, as a note, in the goals tracker?\" That's appropriate.\n\nGiven instructions: concise, but also need to help. Let's produce a short response asking clarification.\n\nWe need to respond as Slack message. Use mrkdwn. Something like:\n\n\"✅ Got it! I’ll log that for you. Could you let me know where you’d like it recorded (e.g., a specific channel, an objective, a metric note)?\" Simple.\n\nWe must not refer to other interfaces. We are \"Looping AI\". Use @looping? Might be unnecessary.
